description: Interpretive structure over texts — for readers of the Classics, scripture, law, or any corpus where the work is deciding what a passage MEANS. Passages (snippets) carry readings: a commentary passage #Interprets a primary passage; rival readings each #Interprets the same target and argue among themselves with argument-diagramming's #Supports/#Opposes (frameworks compose — a reading is just a passage, so the whole argument toolkit applies to interpretive disputes). #Tension keeps an apparent conflict between passages ON the record without resolving it — resolution, if any, is a reading that #Interprets each side. Work-level relations (a commentary on a work, a translation of a work) ride source↔source LINKs. The fourfold sense (quadriga) is a LEARNER-overlay metadata vocabulary on ANNOTATES — which sense a reading operates in is your judgment about your reading, not a fact about the text (D8: overlay edges only).

edgeTags.name: Interprets
edgeTags.description: this passage offers a reading of that one — what it takes the target to mean. Rival readings each carry their own #Interprets edge; their dispute is #Supports/#Opposes between the readings

edgeTags.name: ParallelPassage
edgeTags.description: these passages tell the same episode or teaching in different places — synoptic parallels, doublets, recensions

edgeTags.name: Alludes
edgeTags.description: this passage echoes or evokes that one — intertextual reference the author expects the reader to hear

edgeTags.name: TypeOf
edgeTags.description: typological reading: this passage prefigures that one (the type points to the antitype). A hermeneutic CLAIM by the tagger, not a property of the text

edgeTags.name: Tension
edgeTags.description: these passages appear to conflict — kept on the record, not resolved; a harmonizing reading #Interprets each side

edgeTags.name: CommentaryOn
edgeTags.description: this work is a sustained commentary on that one

edgeTags.name: TranslationOf
edgeTags.description: this work renders that one in another language — sharper than core's #DerivativeOf when the tradition of translation is itself the object of study
